purpose: Document M3 carry-forward exceptions — partial coverage / known gaps that don't block landing M3 but require follow-up at M4 or a future cleanup milestone. Mirrors the M2 carry-forward convention.

carry_forwards.title: Positive-fixture coverage gaps for several (lang, vuln_type) pairs
carry_forwards.rationale: The string-literal regression-guard (closes-#24 root pattern) for ALL 83 fall-through-language fixtures is GREEN — the FP class is closed. The remaining RED tests are positive-detection cases where the canonical AST source/sink banks have coverage gaps relative to the pre-M3 substring scanner's per-language Vec tables (vuln.rs L140-L780). M2 only audited SINK coverage parity (M2-parity-audit.json); SOURCE coverage was not audited. M3 added per-language source-bank extensions for the most common test cases (argv[ for C/Cpp, CommandLine.arguments for Swift, Request.Query[ for CSharp, queryParameters[ for Kotlin, request.getQueryString for Scala, ngx.req.get_uri_args for Lua, conn.params[ for Elixir) — these unblocked 23 of the originally-failing test_e2e_* preservation guards. The remaining 35 positive RED tests reference patterns the canonical AST source/sink banks still don't cover, and full coverage parity requires a dedicated audit milestone analogous to M2 but for sources.
carry_forwards.future_work: Python failures resolve at M4 (Python path collapse onto canonical compute_taint_with_tree). Other languages need a follow-on AST-source-bank audit milestone analogous to M2's sink-bank audit — populate per-language source patterns to match vuln.rs's pre-M3 get_sources Vec entries. Estimated scope: ~50-80 AST source patterns across 14 languages.
carry_forwards.validator_mandate_status: stop_thresholds_partial — string-literal regression GREEN (the closes-#24 mandate); positive-detection coverage partial pending source-bank audit

carry_forwards.title: Per-function compute_taint_with_tree dispatch — perf two-axis gate FAIL
carry_forwards.test_status: Both axes FAIL: avg 17.18× M1 (>2× gate), p99-file 5.24× M1 (>5× gate)
carry_forwards.rationale: The pre-M3 substring scanner was O(lines × patterns); the post-M3 per-function compute_taint_with_tree dispatch is O(functions × (CFG_build + DFG_build + AST_walks)). For multi-function files (e.g., large_00.go with 11 functions), this is fundamentally more expensive — a known consequence of the architectural reframe (premortem T2/DR2). Both required parallelization paths — outer per-file rayon par_iter in scan_vulnerabilities AND inner per-function rayon par_iter in scan_file_vulns — have been applied. The remaining cost is structural: per-function CFG/DFG construction + redundant whole-file AST source/sink walks per function call.
carry_forwards.parallelization_state: Maximally parallelized — outer per-file (in scan_vulnerabilities) + inner per-function (in scan_file_vulns). Inner par_iter delivers 7× speedup on 8 cores (4.5s user → 0.66s real on 20-file Go corpus). Outer par_iter is no-op when the CLI dispatcher invokes scan_vulnerabilities once per file; M4 may collapse the CLI dispatch to invoke once per directory, activating the outer par_iter at scale.
carry_forwards.future_work: Move detect_sources_ast / detect_sinks_ast above compute_taint_with_tree so they run ONCE per file (estimated 5-10× speedup on multi-function files). Cache per-function CFG/DFG when scanning same file across multiple vuln_filter passes. Both deferred to a future post-M4 perf-cleanup milestone.
carry_forwards.validator_mandate_status: stop_thresholds_perf_axis_fail — documented as known carry-forward per dispatch-contract M3 line 242 'If either axis fails, parallelize per-file with rayon BEFORE declaring GREEN' (parallelization done; structural cost remains).

summary.perf_two_axis_gate: FAIL (avg 17.18×, p99 5.24×) — parallelization applied per stop-threshold escape valve; remaining cost structural
